name: Macaroon Jam Slices
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Lightly butter a 14-by-17-inch baking sheet with a pastry brush or paper towel. Sprinkle some flour over it, tip the sheet from side to side to cover the surface evenly, then knock off the excess by inverting the sheet and tapping it against the edge of a table.
3. In a large mixing bowl, combine the ground walnuts and sugar. Add half of the slightly beaten egg white, and stir until the mixture forms a paste thick enough to be kneaded with your hand. If the mixture seems too dry, add a little more of the second egg white. Shape the paste into a roll about 3/4-inch in diameter and 10-inches long.
4. Using the index finger, make a channel about 1/2-inch at each end of the roll.
5. Lift the rolls with a large metal spatula and place them several inches apart on the baking sheet.
6. Bake for 15 or 20 minutes, or until lightly browned. Meanwhile, heat the jam in a saucepan until it runs easily off the spoon.
7. Remove the rolls from the oven and fill the channels with the hot jam. Let cool.
8. To serve, cut the rolls into slices 3/4-inch thick.
